growth_records.cite: Tan KH et al. Porphyromonas gingivalis and Treponema denticola Exhibit Metabolic Symbioses. PLoS Pathogens (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.ppat.1003955. [PMC3946380]
growth_records.snippet: The three T. denticola mono-cultures also reached steady state approximately 8 days after inoculation with an average A650 nm of 0.23±0.02 equating to 7.7±0.7×10^8 cells mL-1
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D = 39 mL/h / 900 mL = 0.0433 h-1 (reported 0.044 h-1); doubling time = reported mean generation time 15.75 h (= ln2/0.044). No uptake/secretion rates measured in this chemostat condition; glycine catabolism was quantified separately in batch (see companion record). pH not stated; OBGM composition cited from ref 27 not given.

sec.units: mM (likely underestimate due to CO2 equilibration)

mfa.note: ~73% of [U-13C]glycine carbon incorporated into acetate; majority of remainder into lactate; 13C metabolic-labeling fermentation

growth_records.cite: Tan KH et al. Porphyromonas gingivalis and Treponema denticola Exhibit Metabolic Symbioses. PLoS Pathogens (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.ppat.1003955. [PMC3946380]
growth_records.snippet: [U-13C]glycine was completely consumed by 144 h, with production of 13C-acetate (3.67 mM) and 13C-lactate (1 mM) ... Catabolism of 13C-glycine was also associated with the production of H13CO3. The yields of H13CO3 (0.17 mM)
growth_records.notes: Batch metabolic-labeling fermentation (priority measured record). 5.00 mM [U-13C]glycine added at 24 h, fully consumed by 144 h => consumed over ~120 h; crude volumetric uptake = 5.00 mM / 120 h = 0.0417 mM/h (=0.0417 mmol/L/h) — biomass in gDW not reported so mmol/gDW/h not derivable. Product yields computed from explicit paper values: acetate 3.67/5.00=0.734, lactate 1.0/5.00=0.20, bicarbonate 0.17/5.00=0.034 mol/mol. Text also states ~73% of labeled glycine carbon incorporated into acetate. Separate suspension experiment: 1.46 mM glycine in OBGM rapidly depleted over 72 h; 10 mM glycine supplementation raised final cell density 1.75-fold. Products measured by 13C-NMR (Metabolomics Australia). 37C anaerobic; OBGM full composition not given.
